rubric.description: The skill clearly states its purpose and has explicit 'When to Use' and 'When Not to Use' sections that are specific, not vague
The skill documents its inputs (required vs optional) and any prerequisites (tools, SDK versions, etc.)
The skill provides a numbered, sequential workflow with concrete steps and commands, not generic guidance
The skill includes validation steps, success criteria, or checklists that let the agent verify progress and completion
The skill documents common failure modes, pitfalls, or edge cases with specific recovery guidance
The skill includes concrete code snippets, commands, or examples rather than abstract descriptions
The writing is concise, specific, and unambiguous with no clever wording that could be misread by an agent
The skill is explicit about which tools and commands to use and does not assume unavailable or proprietary tooling
